name: Asparagus Cornet with Lemon Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. For the pastry cornets:
- Roll the pastry out onto a lightly floured work surface to 3-inch x 20-inch rectangle, 1/4-inch thick.
- Cut the rectangle into 4 long strips, each 3/4-inch wide.
- Wrap each strip around a long metal cone (made from heavy-duty aluminum foil), beginning at the tip and rolling the strip around and around, overlapping slightly. Place these on an ungreased baking sheet and refrigerate for 1 hour or more.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F.
3. Bake pastry cornets until golden, about 15 minutes. Cool briefly, then carefully remove the metal or foil cones.
4. For the lemon dressing:
- Combine the egg yolks, mustard, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Whisk in the oil a drop at a time.
- When the dressing begins to thicken, add the oil 1 teaspoon at a time, then continue adding oil in a thin stream.
- Adjust seasonings to taste.
5. Cook the asparagus, tied in bundles, in boiling salted water until it's just crisp-tender; drain.
Construction:
- Pour a small amount of dressing on each serving plate.
- Place a cornet on top of the dressing and tuck the asparagus into the ends of the cornet, fanning the tips outward.
